executive_summary: Add a pre-merge gate that detects test/code call-surface misalignment when a PR removes a phantom-table reference but leaves orphaned tests directory assertions referencing the removed table name. The gate runs at LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL alongside wire-check-gate and fails (score=0) with a bracket-tokenized diagnostic when at least one removed-table string literal is referenced in a test file that is NOT also edited in the same PR. Also UPSERTs the PAT-PHANTOM-TABLE-TEST-MISALIGNMENT-001 issue_patterns row (CAPA-2) cross-linked to the just-closed PAT-LEO-INFRA-WRITER-CONSUMER-ASYMMETRY-001 (via metadata.related_patterns, not a top-level column — empirical schema verification by LEAD VALIDATION row 7abcad58 and RISK row 33844bdd). Scope-locked to CAPA-2 + CAPA-3 only; CAPA-4 (triage-baseline-failures.js + baseline-allowlist for test_failures gate budget) is deferred to a follow-up SD. Origin: feedback 9f24c164-471a-4f0c-a506-4d5762c52a55 (RCA cluster from QF-20260509-849). All factory shape and validator return shape decisions empirically pinned to wire-check-gate.js conventions per LEAD VALIDATION evidence.

functional_requirements.title: Audit script — pure-function core auditPhantomTableTests({removedTables, changedFiles, testFiles}) returning {passed, issues, details}
functional_requirements.description: Create scripts/phantom-test-audit.js implementing the audit logic as a PURE FUNCTION with array inputs: auditPhantomTableTests({removedTables: Set<string>, changedFiles: string[], testFiles: Array<{path:string, contents:string}>}) returning {passed: boolean, issues: string[], details: {orphaned_tests: Array<{table:string, testFile:string}>, matched_edits: string[]}}. Pure-function shape is the testing-agent recommendation (evidence row 790e7750) — eliminates execSync mocking, makes the audit script trivially unit-testable with array fixtures while the gate wrapper handles all git I/O. Script is ALSO CLI-invokable: CLI wrapper at the bottom of the file uses execSync('git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD') + execSync('git diff origin/main..HEAD') to collect inputs and calls the pure function, then process.exit(result.passed ? 0 : 1) with diagnostic on stderr.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/phantom-test-audit.js exists and is CLI-invokable
Exports named function auditPhantomTableTests({removedTables, changedFiles, testFiles}) as ES module export
Pure function: NO execSync, NO fs, NO git inside auditPhantomTableTests — all I/O lives in the CLI wrapper at the bottom of the file
Returns {passed: boolean, issues: string[], details: {orphaned_tests, matched_edits}}
CLI wrapper exit code: 0 when passed=true, 1 when passed=false; diagnostic on stderr
Header docstring documents the limitation: single-line single/double-quoted string literals only (no template literals, no .from(${var}) dynamic refs, no multi-line strings)

functional_requirements.title: Gate wrapper createPhantomTestAuditGate following wire-check-gate factory conventions verbatim
functional_requirements.description: Create scripts/modules/handoff/executors/lead-final-approval/gates/phantom-test-audit-gate.js exporting createPhantomTestAuditGate({...}) factory. Empirically pinned to wire-check-gate.js:151-296 conventions (LEAD VALIDATION evidence row 7abcad58): returns {name: 'PHANTOM_TEST_AUDIT', validator: async(ctx) => ({passed, score, max_score, issues, warnings, details}), required: true}. NOT {name, validate} as the SD's enrichment text incorrectly stated. The gate's validator does the git work: extract removed table-name string literals via git diff origin/main..HEAD parsing, enumerate test files recursively from tests/ and __tests__/, parse commit subjects for /phantom.*table|dead.*query/i trigger, and call auditPhantomTableTests with the collected arrays. Early-return passed=true with score=100 when zero commit subjects match the trigger regex (cost ≈ 0 for 99% of SDs — RISK row 33844bdd empirical: 1/2000 origin/main subjects match, all genuine).
acceptance_criteria: File at scripts/modules/handoff/executors/lead-final-approval/gates/phantom-test-audit-gate.js
Exports createPhantomTestAuditGate({...}) factory function
Returned gate object has shape {name: 'PHANTOM_TEST_AUDIT', validator: async(ctx) => ({...}), required: true}
Validator returns the canonical handoff-gate verdict shape: {passed: boolean, score: 0|100, max_score: 100, issues: string[], warnings: string[], details: object}

Early-return when no commit subject in origin/main..HEAD matches /phantom.*table|dead.*query/i (subject-only, NOT body — RISK recommendation: subject-only is empirically sufficient and avoids future retro-cite false-positives)

Bypass mechanism: when env PHANTOM_TEST_AUDIT_BYPASS=1 OR sd.metadata.governance_metadata.bypass_reason contains 'PHANTOM_TEST_AUDIT_BYPASS', emit warnings entry and return passed=true (allows escape hatch for confirmed false-positives)
Gate failure message includes the bypass invocation text inline (operator-discoverability per RISK ADVISORY)

functional_requirements.title: Gate registration in gates.js — three insertion sites mirror wire-check-gate precedent
functional_requirements.description: Register the new gate in scripts/modules/handoff/executors/lead-final-approval/gates.js. Three insertion sites per LEAD VALIDATION empirical finding (rows 7abcad58, 33844bdd): (i) lines 33-35: import + named re-export pattern (mirror line 34-35 wire-check-gate import); (ii) line 1185: push in getRequiredGates() function alongside other always-required gates; (iii) lines 1202-1218: default-export map entry. All three sites must be modified atomically in the same commit for the gate to register cleanly into the LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL gate batch. Single-line shape for all three; no auxiliary helper files needed (RISK warning [WARN:SUB_CLAIM_GAP] — wire-check-gate added lib/static-analysis/* helpers because of AST complexity; this gate is regex+fs+git only, no helper extraction needed).
acceptance_criteria: gates.js contains import line: import {createPhantomTestAuditGate} from './gates/phantom-test-audit-gate.js'
gates.js contains named re-export line: export {createPhantomTestAuditGate}
gates.js getRequiredGates() includes phantom-test-audit-gate in the always-required list
gates.js default-export map (lines 1202-1218 region) has entry for PHANTOM_TEST_AUDIT pointing to the factory
No new package.json dependencies (no fast-glob, no glob, no minimatch — use Node 20+ fs.readdir({recursive:true}))
No new lib/static-analysis/* helper files (RISK [WARN:SUB_CLAIM_GAP] confirmation: this gate is INLINE-ONLY)

functional_requirements.title: Unit tests for audit logic — pure-function tests with array fixtures (no git I/O in test code)
functional_requirements.description: Create tests/unit/handoff/gates/phantom-test-audit-gate.test.js (canonical repo location per LEAD VALIDATION empirical: wire-check-gate.test.js lives at tests/unit/handoff/wire-check-gate.test.js). Six test scenarios required: TS-1 (audit FAIL with phantom commit + orphaned test fixture), TS-2 (audit PASS early-return when no phantom commit), TS-3 (audit PASS when phantom commit AND same-PR test edit), TS-4 (gates.js static-pin: import + named re-export + getRequiredGates inclusion), TS-5 (issue_patterns DB-touching skip-if-no-key: row exists with metadata.related_patterns cross-link), TS-6 (gate validator returns canonical {passed, score, max_score, issues, warnings, details} shape on both PASS and FAIL paths). TS-1/TS-2/TS-3 use pure-function array fixtures (no execSync, no tmpdir — testing-agent recommendation row 790e7750: Option B canonical contract). TS-4 uses fs.readFileSync + regex on gates.js (mirror sd-transition-readiness-allowlist test pattern). TS-5 uses createSupabaseServiceClient with skip-if-undefined env guard.
acceptance_criteria: Test file at tests/unit/handoff/gates/phantom-test-audit-gate.test.js

TS-1: audit({removedTables:new Set(['sd_validation_results']), changedFiles:['src/foo.js'], testFiles:[{path:'tests/foo.test.js', contents:"expect.from('sd_validation_results')..."}]}) → passed=false, issues.length>0, details.orphaned_tests includes {table:'sd_validation_results', testFile:'tests/foo.test.js'}
TS-2: audit({removedTables:new Set(), changedFiles:[], testFiles:[]}) → passed=true (early-return semantic at audit-fn level)

TS-3: audit({removedTables:new Set(['sd_validation_results']), changedFiles:['src/foo.js', 'tests/foo.test.js'], testFiles:[{path:'tests/foo.test.js', contents:"... 'sd_validation_results' ..."}]}) → passed=true, details.matched_edits includes 'tests/foo.test.js' (NOT just passed=true — must verify the match was recognized)

TS-4: gates.js source string contains both /import\s*\{[^}]*createPhantomTestAuditGate[^}]*\}/ AND /export\s*\{[^}]*createPhantomTestAuditGate[^}]*\}/ AND a string literal 'PHANTOM_TEST_AUDIT' in the getRequiredGates region

TS-5: skip-if-no SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY; otherwise query issue_patterns by pattern_id='PAT-PHANTOM-TABLE-TEST-MISALIGNMENT-001' → exactly 1 row, row.metadata.related_patterns array contains 'PAT-LEO-INFRA-WRITER-CONSUMER-ASYMMETRY-001', row.category='infrastructure', row.status='active'

TS-6: invoke createPhantomTestAuditGate()'s validator with mocked context for PASS path AND FAIL path; assert toEqual({passed:true, score:100, max_score:100, issues:[], warnings:[], details:{...}}) for PASS and toEqual({passed:false, score:0, max_score:100, issues:[/phantom/], warnings:[], details:{orphaned_tests:[...]}}) for FAIL
Test LOC budget: 180-240 across all 6 (per testing-agent revised estimate row 790e7750)

functional_requirements.title: Issue_patterns UPSERT — closes CAPA-2 with empirically-correct schema
functional_requirements.description: Create scripts/one-off/_seed-issue-pattern-phantom-table-001.mjs that UPSERTs the PAT-PHANTOM-TABLE-TEST-MISALIGNMENT-001 row into issue_patterns using the canonical createSupabaseServiceClient helper. Schema correction per LEAD VALIDATION + RISK empirical: NO top-level related_patterns column, NO top-level title column. Cross-link goes in metadata.related_patterns (jsonb array of pattern_id strings); title goes in issue_summary. Use upsert({...}, {onConflict:'pattern_id'}) for idempotency. Set status='active', category='infrastructure', severity='medium', occurrence_count=1, source_feedback_ids=['9f24c164-471a-4f0c-a506-4d5762c52a55'], metadata={related_patterns:['PAT-LEO-INFRA-WRITER-CONSUMER-ASYMMETRY-001'], rca_source:'QF-20260509-849', capa_coverage:['CAPA-2','CAPA-3']}.
acceptance_criteria: File at scripts/one-off/_seed-issue-pattern-phantom-table-001.mjs
Uses createSupabaseServiceClient (NOT direct supabase-js with anon key)
upsert call uses {onConflict:'pattern_id'} for idempotency

Row fields set: pattern_id='PAT-PHANTOM-TABLE-TEST-MISALIGNMENT-001', category='infrastructure', severity='medium', status='active', issue_summary=<description>, occurrence_count=1, source_feedback_ids=['9f24c164-471a-4f0c-a506-4d5762c52a55']
metadata.related_patterns is a jsonb array containing 'PAT-LEO-INFRA-WRITER-CONSUMER-ASYMMETRY-001'
metadata.rca_source='QF-20260509-849' and metadata.capa_coverage=['CAPA-2','CAPA-3']
Script idempotent: re-running produces no duplicate row, no error

functional_requirements.title: Negative-control test pinning subject-only detection (avoids false-positives on adjacent phantom-* commits)
functional_requirements.description: Extend tests/unit/handoff/gates/phantom-test-audit-gate.test.js with negative-control assertions per RISK ADVISORY [WARN:BODY_SCAN_FUTURE_CREEP]: confirm the trigger regex /phantom.*table|dead.*query/i is applied to commit SUBJECTS ONLY, not bodies. Empirical RISK evidence row 33844bdd: against origin/main's 2000 commits, subject-only scanning produces exactly 1 match (genuine target edb93d8d3b); body scanning would future-cite retros that mention the RCA pattern and create false-positives. Test pins this by passing fixtures with subjects containing 'phantom-completion-sweep', 'phantom worktree', 'phantom-detect', 'NODE_MODULES_LOCK phantom protocol' — none should trigger the audit. ALSO pins that bodies containing /phantom.*table/ in a retro-style cite DO NOT trigger.
acceptance_criteria: TS-2 extended OR new TS-2b with parameterized fixtures: ['phantom-completion-sweep', 'phantom worktree cleanup', 'phantom-detect QF-XX', 'NODE_MODULES_LOCK phantom protocol'] all yield passed=true (early-return)

TS-2c: fixture with commit subject NOT matching trigger but commit BODY containing 'this references the phantom table pattern from earlier RCA' → audit early-returns passed=true (body NOT scanned for trigger)

system_architecture.overview: Three new files (audit script + gate wrapper + seed script) + one edit (gates.js 3-site registration). Audit script is pure-function for testability; gate wrapper does all git I/O. Issue_patterns row is seeded via one-off mjs using canonical service-role client. No new dependencies, no helper extraction, no migration. All file paths empirically anchored to existing wire-check-gate precedent (LEAD VALIDATION row 7abcad58).
components: scripts/phantom-test-audit.js (audit logic, pure function + CLI wrapper)
scripts/modules/handoff/executors/lead-final-approval/gates.js (3-site registration: import, re-export, getRequiredGates push, default-export map entry)
system_architecture.data_flow: 1) LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL handoff invokes gate batch via gates.js getRequiredGates(). 2) Gate validator extracts commit subjects in origin/main..HEAD range; early-returns passed=true (score=100) if zero match /phantom.*table|dead.*query/i. 3) On match: gate validator parses git diff for removed single-line single/double-quoted string literals, enumerates test files recursively, identifies orphaned references, and checks changedFiles via git diff --name-only. 4) Gate validator calls auditPhantomTableTests pure function with the collected arrays. 5) Function returns {passed, issues, details}; gate wraps in canonical handoff-gate verdict shape and emits diagnostic message including bypass invocation text. 6) LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL aggregates the verdict into the gate batch result.
integration_points: issue_patterns table (UPSERT target; cross-link via metadata.related_patterns)
Existing PAT-LEO-INFRA-WRITER-CONSUMER-ASYMMETRY-001 row in issue_patterns (cross-link target, exists with occurrence_count=5)

technical_requirements.title: Factory shape mirrors wire-check-gate verbatim
technical_requirements.description: Returned gate object: {name: 'PHANTOM_TEST_AUDIT', validator: async(ctx)=>{...}, required: true}. NOT {name, validate}. Empirical anchor: scripts/modules/handoff/executors/lead-final-approval/gates/wire-check-gate.js:151-296 (LEAD VALIDATION evidence row 7abcad58).

technical_requirements.title: Validator return shape mirrors wire-check-gate verbatim
technical_requirements.description: {passed: boolean, score: 0|100, max_score: 100, issues: string[], warnings: string[], details: object}. Empirical anchor: wire-check-gate.js:189-296.

technical_requirements.title: issue_patterns cross-link via metadata.related_patterns (NOT top-level)
technical_requirements.description: Schema empirically verified: no top-level related_patterns column, no top-level title column. Cross-link stored in metadata jsonb as related_patterns array of pattern_id strings (LEAD VALIDATION row 7abcad58, RISK row 33844bdd). Update SD success_metric TS-5 wording to assert metadata.related_patterns (already done in PRD acceptance_criteria FR-4 TS-5).

technical_requirements.title: Subject-only regex scan, NOT body scan
technical_requirements.description: Trigger /phantom.*table|dead.*query/i applied to commit subjects only. Empirical RISK evidence (row 33844bdd): 1/2000 origin/main subjects match (genuine target edb93d8d3b); body scanning would create future false-positives via retro-cites. Pin via FR-6 negative-control tests.

technical_requirements.title: Pure-function audit + git I/O in gate wrapper (Option B)
technical_requirements.description: auditPhantomTableTests({removedTables, changedFiles, testFiles}) is pure (no execSync, no fs, no git). Gate wrapper collects inputs via git log/git diff/fs.readdir and passes arrays. Testing-agent recommendation (row 790e7750): eliminates execSync mocking anti-pattern; trivially unit-testable with array fixtures.

technical_requirements.title: Node 20+ fs.readdir({recursive:true}) for test enumeration
technical_requirements.description: Repo uses Node v24.12.0; native recursive readdir is available. No fast-glob dependency needed (LEAD VALIDATION row 7abcad58 finding).

technical_requirements.title: Canonical test file path
technical_requirements.description: tests/unit/handoff/gates/phantom-test-audit-gate.test.js — mirrors tests/unit/handoff/wire-check-gate.test.js convention (LEAD VALIDATION empirical row 7abcad58). NOT flat tests/ root.

technical_requirements.title: vitest pool: forks default — no override
technical_requirements.description: Repo default at vitest.config.js:30 (testing-agent row 790e7750). Tests use mkdtempSync + afterEach cleanup for fork safety.

technical_requirements.title: Bypass mechanism: env + sd.metadata.governance_metadata.bypass_reason
technical_requirements.description: Two paths: env PHANTOM_TEST_AUDIT_BYPASS=1 OR sd.metadata.governance_metadata.bypass_reason containing 'PHANTOM_TEST_AUDIT_BYPASS' token. Gate emits warning entry and returns passed=true. Operator-discoverable: failure message includes bypass invocation text inline (RISK ADVISORY).

technical_requirements.title: Idempotent UPSERT in seed script
technical_requirements.description: upsert({...}, {onConflict:'pattern_id'}) for the issue_patterns row. Re-running the seed produces no duplicate and no error. createSupabaseServiceClient helper handles service-role auth.

test_scenarios: TS-1 (audit FAIL, FR-1+FR-4): pure-function call with removedTables={sd_validation_results}, changedFiles=['src/foo.js'], testFiles=[{path:'tests/foo.test.js', contents:"expect.from('sd_validation_results')"}] → passed=false, issues includes phantom diagnostic, details.orphaned_tests=[{table:'sd_validation_results', testFile:'tests/foo.test.js'}]
TS-2 (audit early-return PASS, FR-1+FR-4): pure-function call with empty arrays → passed=true, issues=[]

TS-2b (subject-only negative control, FR-6): gate validator (mocked git log) with commit subjects ['phantom-completion-sweep cleanup', 'phantom worktree reap', 'phantom-detect QF-X', 'NODE_MODULES_LOCK phantom protocol'] → passed=true (early-return, none trigger)
TS-2c (body-not-scanned, FR-6): gate validator (mocked git log) with subject='feat: unrelated change' but body='see RCA on phantom table issue' → passed=true (body not scanned for trigger)

TS-3 (audit PASS with same-PR test edit, FR-1+FR-4): pure-function call with removedTables={sd_validation_results}, changedFiles=['src/foo.js', 'tests/foo.test.js'], testFiles=[{path:'tests/foo.test.js', contents:"... 'sd_validation_results' ..."}] → passed=true, details.matched_edits includes 'tests/foo.test.js'

TS-4 (gate registration static-pin, FR-3+FR-4): fs.readFileSync(gates.js, 'utf8') — assert (a) import line matches /import\s*\{[^}]*createPhantomTestAuditGate[^}]*\}/, (b) re-export line matches /export\s*\{[^}]*createPhantomTestAuditGate[^}]*\}/, (c) 'PHANTOM_TEST_AUDIT' string literal present in getRequiredGates region

TS-5 (issue_patterns row + cross-link, FR-5+FR-4): skip-if-no-SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY; otherwise query by pattern_id='PAT-PHANTOM-TABLE-TEST-MISALIGNMENT-001' → exactly 1 row, metadata.related_patterns contains 'PAT-LEO-INFRA-WRITER-CONSUMER-ASYMMETRY-001', category='infrastructure', status='active'

TS-6 (gate verdict shape pin, FR-2+FR-4): instantiate createPhantomTestAuditGate(), invoke validator with mocked ctx for PASS and FAIL paths, assert toEqual on canonical {passed, score, max_score, issues, warnings, details} shape with score=100 on PASS and score=0 on FAIL
implementation_approach: Single-PR ship in EXEC. (1) Author scripts/phantom-test-audit.js with pure-function auditPhantomTableTests + CLI wrapper (~120 LOC). (2) Author scripts/modules/handoff/executors/lead-final-approval/gates/phantom-test-audit-gate.js mirroring wire-check-gate factory verbatim (~50 LOC). (3) Edit gates.js at 3 insertion sites: import (line ~35), named re-export (line ~36), getRequiredGates push (line ~1185), default-export map (lines ~1202-1218) — actual line numbers may shift; use regex anchoring. (4) Author tests/unit/handoff/gates/phantom-test-audit-gate.test.js with 8 test scenarios (TS-1, TS-2, TS-2b, TS-2c, TS-3, TS-4, TS-5, TS-6) — ~180-240 LOC. (5) Author scripts/one-off/_seed-issue-pattern-phantom-table-001.mjs and RUN it to UPSERT the issue_patterns row before commit. (6) Run vitest on the new test file; iterate to PASS. (7) Run a self-validation: invoke handoff.js LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL on a known-clean branch (or use a test SD) and confirm the gate registers + passes. (8) Commit + push + PR.
acceptance_criteria: FR-1 through FR-6 all complete with per-FR acceptance criteria met
All 8 test scenarios (TS-1, TS-2, TS-2b, TS-2c, TS-3, TS-4, TS-5, TS-6) PASS in vitest
TS-5 specifically: issue_patterns query returns exactly 1 row with metadata.related_patterns containing PAT-LEO-INFRA-WRITER-CONSUMER-ASYMMETRY-001
Gate registers successfully into LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL handoff (no regression on existing gate tests; wire-check-gate.test.js still passes)
scripts/one-off/_seed-issue-pattern-phantom-table-001.mjs is committed AND has been run successfully (issue_patterns row written)
No new package.json dependencies added (Node 20+ native fs.readdir{recursive:true} used)
No new lib/static-analysis/* helper files (INLINE-ONLY confirmation per RISK [WARN:SUB_CLAIM_GAP])
Subject-only regex scan confirmed (TS-2b + TS-2c negative controls PASS)
Test file LOC within 180-240 budget (testing-agent recommendation)
Closes feedback 9f24c164-471a-4f0c-a506-4d5762c52a55 via PR footer 'Closes feedback 9f24c164-471a-4f0c-a506-4d5762c52a55'

risks.risk: Trigger regex false-positive on adjacent phantom-* commits (phantom-completion-sweep, phantom worktrees, etc.)
risks.mitigation: Subject-only scan (empirical: 1/2000 false-positives in origin/main per RISK row 33844bdd). FR-6 negative-control tests pin this. Bypass env + governance_metadata escape hatch documented in failure message.

risks.risk: Diff parsing limitation: multi-line strings, template literals, .from(${var}) dynamic refs are NOT covered
risks.mitigation: Header docstring documents the limitation. Gate failure message surfaces it (operator can't misread absence-of-failure as absence-of-misalignment). Same-PR test edit check is the secondary safety net.

risks.risk: gates.js 3-site registration is fragile — line numbers shift
risks.mitigation: Use regex anchoring in EXEC (NOT line numbers). TS-4 static-pin asserts presence of import/re-export/PHANTOM_TEST_AUDIT literal via regex, not line offsets. Future drift surfaces as test failure.

risks.risk: issue_patterns schema drift (related_patterns column added as top-level in future)
risks.mitigation: FR-5 uses metadata.related_patterns (canonical today). If schema migrates to top-level, FR-5 still works (jsonb metadata is preserved). TS-5 specifically asserts metadata path, not top-level.

risks.risk: CAPA-4 deferral creates baseline-failures ledger requirement gap
risks.mitigation: Open CAPA-4 follow-up SD (DRAFT) before LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL of this SD (LEAD VALIDATION row 7abcad58 recommendation). Cross-link in retrospective.

risks.risk: Verdict enum constraint surprise (only PASS/FAIL/BLOCKED accepted)
risks.mitigation: Gate emits {passed: false, score: 0, issues: [...]} on FAIL — handoff pipeline maps to BLOCKED/FAIL based on required:true. No verdict enum interaction needed at gate level. Sub-agent evidence rows (LEAD/PLAN/EXEC) use PASS/FAIL only per empirical constraint (RISK row 33844bdd).

integration_operationalization.consumers: Internal LEO Protocol consumers only. Every SD's LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL handoff invokes the gate batch; phantom-test-audit-gate runs alongside wire-check-gate. SDs whose feature branch contains a commit subject matching /phantom.*table|dead.*query/i are scanned for test/code misalignment. SDs whose branches have NO matching commit get a near-zero-cost early-return pass. No customer-facing UI, no end-user feature, no API change.

dependencies.name: createSupabaseServiceClient helper
dependencies.failure_mode: Seed script fails loud if SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY undefined. CLI exits non-zero; no partial UPSERT.

dependencies.name: issue_patterns table schema
dependencies.failure_mode: If schema migrates to remove metadata column, FR-5 UPSERT fails. Mitigation: TS-5 catches this in CI before merge.

dependencies.name: wire-check-gate.js factory shape
dependencies.failure_mode: If wire-check-gate refactors away from {name, validator, required} shape, our gate diverges. TS-6 pins our gate's shape; doesn't pin wire-check-gate's.

dependencies.name: gates.js getRequiredGates / default-export map
dependencies.failure_mode: If registration pattern changes (e.g., gates auto-discovered via fs.readdir), our 3-site edit becomes unnecessary but harmless. TS-4 static-pin would fail loud and signal the refactor.

integration_operationalization.data_contracts: Single read+write contract: read commit subjects + git diff vs origin/main + test file contents (fs); write 1 issue_patterns row via UPSERT. No schema changes. No new tables. No new columns. No new RLS policies.
integration_operationalization.runtime_config: Env vars: PHANTOM_TEST_AUDIT_BYPASS=1 (bypass). Optional: PHANTOM_TEST_AUDIT_TRIGGER_REGEX override (default /phantom.*table|dead.*query/i). DISABLE_SSL_VERIFY=true unchanged for local dev. SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY required for FR-5 seed and TS-5.
integration_operationalization.observability_rollout: Gate failure message includes diagnostic with: matched commit SHA + subject, list of removed table literals, list of orphaned test files, bypass invocation text. Rollout: ship gate as required:true from day 1 (RISK ADVISORY recommended dual-mode rollout via ENFORCE_PHANTOM_TEST_AUDIT env flag — accepting that as future enhancement, not P1 scope). Rollback: revert gates.js 3-site registration + delete the new files; issue_patterns row stays (read-only data, no harm).

test_strategy: Tier 1 (CI default): TS-1, TS-2, TS-2b, TS-2c, TS-3, TS-4, TS-6 — fs+pure-function tests, no DB, no env gating. Tier 2 (DB-touching, opt-in): TS-5 — skip-if-no SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY. Tier 3 (canary post-merge): manual invocation of handoff.js LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL on a test SD branch — confirm gate registers and passes/fails on synthetic phantom commits. Test LOC estimate: 180-240 across 8 scenarios. Coverage: audit logic 100% (TS-1/2/3), gate verdict shape 100% (TS-6), registration 100% (TS-4), DB seed 100% (TS-5), negative controls 100% (TS-2b/2c).

success_metrics.metric: Gate detects misalignment in synthetic fixture
Gate passes when test file IS edited in same PR

success_metrics.target: TS-4 PASS — regex assertions on gates.js source
TS-5 PASS or SKIP (no service-role key in CI)
TS-6 PASS — toEqual on {passed, score, max_score, issues, warnings, details}
TS-2b + TS-2c PASS — 4+1 adjacent phantom-* fixtures yield passed=true

success_metrics.metric: Total test count for CAPA-2+CAPA-3 coverage
Zero regression on existing gate tests

metadata.regex_anchor: /phantom.*table|dead.*query/i applied to commit SUBJECTS ONLY (subject-only is empirically sufficient per RISK row 33844bdd: 1/2000 origin/main subjects match, all genuine)
